seo.title: Happy Horse 1.0 | Unified AI Video Model with Native Audio | MaxVideoAI
seo.description: Use Happy Horse 1.0 on MaxVideoAI for text-to-video, image-to-video, R2V reference images, video editing, native audio, and lip-sync.

overview: Happy Horse 1.0 is Alibaba's unified AI video model on MaxVideoAI for text prompts, first-frame images, R2V reference-image control, and video-to-video editing with native synchronized audio and lip-sync.
pricingNotes: Text, image, and R2V runs use standard per-second rates by resolution. Video edit is billed at a combined input/output rate, so it costs double the standard rate for the selected resolution.

hero.title: Happy Horse 1.0 - unified video generation with native audio
hero.intro: Generate from text, animate an image, guide a scene with up to nine reference images, or edit an existing clip from the same Happy Horse model card.
hero.badge: Text · Image · R2V · V2V · 720p/1080p · Native audio · Lip-sync

items.title: Unified text, image, R2V, and V2V production workflows
Video edits with natural-language style direction

technicalOverview.label: Workflows
technicalOverview.body: Text-to-video, image-to-video, R2V reference-image generation, and video edit are exposed as one model in MaxVideoAI.

technicalOverview.label: Duration
technicalOverview.body: 3-15 s for generation outputs; video edit accepts 3-60 s source clips and caps output to the first 15 s.

technicalOverview.label: R2V references
technicalOverview.body: 1-9 images, addressed as character1 through character9 in the prompt.

technicalOverview.label: V2V edit
technicalOverview.body: One source video, optional reference images up to five, and audio handling set to auto or origin.

technicalOverview.label: Audio
technicalOverview.body: Native synchronized audio and lip-sync are treated as part of the generation.

promptStructure.title: Prompting Happy Horse 1.0
promptStructure.quote: Subject + action + camera + audio cue + workflow-specific references.
promptStructure.description: Keep prompts direct and assign each reference a clear role. In R2V, name each image as character1, character2, and so on.
steps: Choose the workflow: text, image, R2V, or V2V
Describe the subject and visible action
Specify native audio or dialogue cues
Name references explicitly when using R2V or V2V

tips.title: Tips and limits
items: Use T2V for fresh ideas and spokesperson-style native audio shots.
Use I2V when a key visual or first frame is already approved.
Use R2V when identity, wardrobe, product shape, or character continuity matters.
Use V2V when the source clip has the right motion but needs a new look or direction.

prompts.title: Native audio launch clip
prompts.prompt: 16:9 studio product launch clip, a confident creator speaks directly to camera with natural lip-sync, warm key light, subtle camera push, clean room tone and soft launch music.

prompts.title: R2V two-character demo
prompts.prompt: Use character1 and character2 from the reference images in a cinematic two-person product demo, consistent wardrobe, stable faces, natural synchronized dialogue.

prompts.title: Video edit refresh
prompts.prompt: Edit the uploaded clip into a premium launch scene: preserve the person and pacing, refresh the lighting to warm studio gold, keep audio natural with synchronized speech.

faqs.q: What inputs does Happy Horse 1.0 support?
faqs.a: MaxVideoAI exposes Happy Horse 1.0 as one model with text-to-video, image-to-video, R2V reference images, and video-to-video edit workflows.
Yes. Happy Horse is treated as a native-audio model with synchronized speech and lip-sync integrated into the generation flow.
Happy Horse video edit is billed at a combined input/output rate, so V2V is double the standard per-second price for the same resolution.

custom.galleryIntro: Review the model page clips for native audio, lip-sync, and video-edit behavior. Comparison pages intentionally stay text/spec focused for this launch.
quickPricingItems: Use 720p for cheaper iteration across text, image, and R2V.
Use 1080p when the shot is ready for review or publication.
Use V2V after the source clip is selected, because video edit has the doubled input/output rate.
custom.relatedSubtitle: Use Happy Horse when native audio, lip-sync, and multiple input workflows matter more than choosing a specialized single-mode model.

relatedItems.title: Seedance 2.0
relatedItems.description: Compare against Seedance when the decision is unified reference control, native audio behavior, and multi-shot generation.

relatedItems.title: Google Veo 3.1
relatedItems.description: Compare against Veo when premium cinematic realism and audio-native output are the main criteria.

custom.finalPara1: Happy Horse 1.0 fits best when the brief needs one model to cover prompt generation, image animation, reference-guided consistency, native audio, lip-sync, and source video editing; choose Seedance or Kling first when raw realism, image quality, and motion are the priority.
custom.finalPara2: For launch work, start with text or R2V to establish the shot, then use V2V when an approved clip needs a directed edit.
